task_completion.description: Locate and view the original mixed-availability draft order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the Drupal Commerce order page containing the mixed items (Sony WH-1000XM5, Logitech MX Master 3S, and Dell product).

task_completion.description: Ensure one order contains only the in-stock items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An order page showing only the items that are currently in-stock (backordered items must be removed from this order).

task_completion.description: Ensure a separate order contains only the backordered items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate order page showing only the items that are backordered (in-stock items must not be present in this order).

task_completion.description: Change the status of the order with in-stock items to placed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The order containing the in-stock items shows a status of 'Placed', 'Completed', or an equivalent non-draft status.

task_completion.description: Keep the order with backordered items as a draft.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The order containing the backordered items shows a status of 'Draft'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Drupal Commerce web interface to split the orders.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Drupal admin panel, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The orders were genuinely modified in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final orders list or success messages reflect the changes, rather than inspect-element modifications.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing direct database updates or backend scripts used to split the order.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original order contains three items: Sony WH-1000XM5 ($348.00), Logitech MX Master 3S ($99.99), and a Dell product ($1,249.99). The VLM must observe the inventory/availability status of these items in the Drupal Commerce UI to determine which are in-stock and which are backordered, then verify they are partitioned correctly into the two final orders.
